"Protocol Version" coincides with what is reported on the /version resource

Deprecated Resources

This contains deprecated resources to avoid cluttering the main document.

/scanners

(removed beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.3.16) .

(WIP) - Scanners are being actively developed see Scanner Migration .

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

A collection of scanner objects.

Scanners may be added or deleted from the system using the standard model methods. In some environments (GoPxL Lite), the system creates an appropriate scanner by default and does not allow the list to be modified.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reading from /scanners will return an array of scanner id's that can be used to then read each scanner.

Example(s): 

read /scanners
read /scanners {"expandLevel" : -1}
(WIP) create

Adds a scanner into the system.

Arguments:

Item Type Description
type string

One of the following:

  • g1
  • g2
  • g3
  • g2xx

Example: 

TBD

Notifications

Event Type Description
(WIP) TBD

TBD

Example: 

TBD

/scanners/(id)

(WIP) - Scanners are being actively developed see Scanner Migration .

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID : auto-generated

A scanner consists of one or more sensors, which worktogether to produce a unified set of scan outputs. These sensors are contained in a child resource.
Both the scanner resource and each child sensor resource contain settings for scanner operation.
There are several different types of scanners, each described by an appropriate schema. The scanner schemes are found on Scanner Schemas (Archived) .

In Gocator Classic terms, a scanner is a "sensor system". All its sensors are "buddied".

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads a specific scanner.

Example: 

TBD
(WIP) delete

Deletes a spsecific scanner from the system.

Arguments:

Item Type Description
type string

One of the following:

  • g1
  • g2
  • g3
  • g2xx

Example: 

TBD

Notifications

Event Type Description
(WIP) TBD

TBD

Example: 

TBD

/scanners/(id)/visibleSensors

Resource Type: N/A

Array of sensor info objects. Note that this isnot a collection resource ; it is a resource that contains an array.

The visibleSensors list will only contain devices that are relevant to the scanner. E.g. non-Gocator devices will not show up for a Gocator. In fact even incompatible Gocator devices may be filtered out, like G2 sensors for a G3 scanner.

Each sensor info object follows the schema below.

Item Type Keywords Description
serialNumber int
Sensor serial number
address string
IP address of the sensor
partNumber string
Sensor part number
version int[]
Sensor version as an array e.g. 5.2.3.4 becomes [5, 2, 3, 4]
state int

Sensor state:

  • 0: Ready
  • 1: Running
  • -1: Conflict Error
buddyable int
  • 2: Connectable
  • -100: Error Buddied
  • -99: Error State
  • -98: Error Version
  • -97: Error Model
  • -96: Error Address
  • -95: Error Missing
  • -94: Error Connection
  • -93: Error Too Many

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the list of visible sensors.

Example: 

TBD

/scanners/(id)/assignedSensors

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

A collection of sensor objects.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reading from /scanners will return an array of sensor id's that can be used to then read each assigned sensor.

Example(s): 

TBD
create

Adds a sensor to the list connected sensors. This is in essence an "assign buddy" command.

Arguments:

Item Type Description
serialNumber int

Serial number. Can be -1 to indicate a placeholder.

Example(s): 

TBD

Notifications

Event Type Description
(WIP) TBD

TBD

Example: 

TBD

/scanners/(id)/assignedSensors/(sensorId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID : Sensor serial number?

Each object is described by one of the schemas documented in this section.

When a sensor is first created, it is "assigned" but not yet connected. In this state, configuration parameters such as exposures are not initialized. In this state, the parameters may have invalid values such as null or NaN, and they should not be modified . Once the sensor connection completes, these parameters will be initialized. A client should check the "_initialized" parameter before presenting the parameters and allowing them to be modified. Note that the parameters and the _initialized parameter itself are persisted in jobs; this means for a saved job, the parameters are valid and modifiable even if the remote device is not connected.

Sensor schemas are found on Sensor Schemas (Archived) .

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads a specific assigned sensor.

Example: 

TBD
delete

Deletes a specific assigned sensor.

The path for this command should be /scanners/(id)/assignedSensors/(serialNumber)

Arguments:

Item Type Description
type string

One of the following:

  • g1
  • g2
  • g3
  • g2xx

Example: 

TBD
(WIP) reassign

Custom command on a sensor. Reassigns a new serial number to a sensor.

Instead of directly modifying the serialNumber property of a sensor object, a command is used because the act of changing a serial number is a significant operation.

Upon invoking this command, the sensor goes into the connecting state, and the schema changes to "generic". This is identical to a newly created sensor object.

Item Type Description
serialNumber int

Serial number. Can be -1 to indicate a placeholder.

Example: 

TBD

Notifications

Event Type Description
(WIP) TBD

TBD

Example: 

TBD

/<someResource>/$commands/<callableResource>

(Callable resources located under $commands have been moved to an equivalent path without the "$", beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.7.6).
(Callable resources located under $commands have been fully removed beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.22.13).

/system/$commands/start
/system/$commands/stop
/system/$commands/snapshot
/system/$commands/simulate

/system/$commands/reset
/system/$commands/factoryRestore
/system/$commands/upgrade

/tools/$commands/clone
/tools/$commands/move
/tools/$commands/reorder
/tools/$commands/resetMetrics

/routines/(routineId)/$commands/continue
/routines/(routineId)/$commands/abort

/jobs/$commands/load
/jobs/$commands/save
/jobs/$commands/copy
/jobs/$commands/rename
/jobs/$commands/setDefault

(For ATF only)
/internal/files/$commands/read
/internal/files/$commands/write

label properties etc.

(The following fields have been deprecated, and replaced with similiar equivalents that are renamed for clarity/purpose beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.9.18).
(The following fields have been fully removed beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.22.11).

Resource path Item Type Description Notes
/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/outputs/(outputId) label string User configurable label of the output replaced with user configurable displayName





/tools/{{id*} label string User configurable tool label,should be unique within the collection.

replaced with user configurable displayName

replaced with user configurable extId

/tools/{id*}/inputs/{inputId*}

label string User configurable tool input label,should be unique within the collection.

replaced with user configurable displayName

replaced with user configurable extId

/tools/{id*}/outputs/{outputId*}

label string User configurable tool output label,should be unique within the collection.

replaced with user configurable displayName

replaced with user configurable extId

/tools/{id*}/actions/{actionId*}

label string User configurable tool action label, should be unique within the collection.

replaced with user configurable displayName

replaced with user configurable extId






/jobs/files/(job)

id string Unique job name. replaced with jobName





/routineTypes typeName string Type name of the routine. replaced with type





/internal/files/(file) id string Unique file name on sensor. replaced with fileName

Tool Measurement and Feature metrics

(The following resource paths have been deprecated, and replaced with similiar equivalents that are renamed for clarity/purpose beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.9.18).

Resource path Item Type Description Notes
tools/{id*}/outputs/<outputLabel>/measurement
label string Various measurement metrics

replaced with tools/{id*}/outputs/(outputId)/measurement

tools/{id*}/outputs/<outputLabel>/pointFeature
tools/{id*}/outputs/<outputLabel>/lineFeature
tools/{id*}/outputs/<outputLabel>/planeFeature
tools/{id*}/outputs/<outputLabel>/circleFeature

label string Various feature metrics

replaced with tools/{id*}/outputs/(outputId)/pointFeature
replaced with tools/{id*}/outputs/(outputId)/lineFeature
replaced with tools/{id*}/outputs/(outputId)/planeFeature
replaced with tools/{id*}/outputs/(outputId)/circleFeature

/dataSources/(id)

(The following resource paths have been deprecated beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.10.15).

Item Type Keywords Description
colorEncoding [readonly] int

(WIP) Color encoding for video messages.

Move into separate schema?

isHdr [readonly] bool

(WIP) Whether or not the message has HDR data (multiple exposures).

Move into separate schema?

/replay/scanners

(The following fields have been deprecated, and replaced with /replay/tracks beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.23.11).

Resource Type: N/A

Contains replay scanners.

Item Type Keywords Description
scanners [readonly] ReplayScannerInfo[]
A list of scanners recorded by the scanner.

ReplayScannerInfo

Item Type Keywords Description
displayName [readonly] culture-specific string
The display name of the scanner.
engineId [readonly] string
Scan engine ID of the data source at the time of recording. This matches the ID used in /scan.
scannerId [readonly] string
Scanner ID of the data source at the time of recording. This matches the ID used in /scan.
dataSources [readonly] ReplayDataSourceInfo[]
A list of data sources in the scanner.

ReplayDataSourceInfo

Item Type Keywords Description
displayName [readonly] culture-specific string
The display name of the data source.
dataSourceId [readonly] string
The data source ID at the time of recording. This matches the ID used in /dataSources.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the scanners properties.

Notifications

Notification
updated

Occurs whenever the properties change, e.g. list of scanners or recorded data sources change

Changes on this occur on system start (call /system/commands/start) when recording is enabled and when the buffer is cleared.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/outputs/(outputId)

(The following field has been deprecated, and replaced with an equivalent 'recordingEnabled' beginning with GoPxL BE 7.0.23.18).

Resource Type: CollectionItem

Item Type Keywords Description
shouldRecord bool

Whether the output should be recorded or not.

Note: This will be replaced by "recordingEnabled" (to be removed in GOS-3180 - Remove shouldRecord flag from scanner output properties. Complete

/scan/*
/scan/(engineId)/*

(The following resources have been changed, and will be replaced with equivalents due to GOS-3512 - BE: Move the collection of scan engines from /scan to /scan/engines Complete as well as minor updates in GOS-3511 - BE: Fix serial number naming inconsistencies in the scanner resource. Complete .

Resource path Item Type Description Notes
/scan various various Collection of scan engines replaced with /scan/engines
/scan/(engineId)/* various various Scan engine resources

replaced with /scan/engines/(engineId)/*

/scan/(engineId)/visibleSensors various various Scan engine visible sensors

available for all scan engines at /scan/visibleSensors
moved to /scan/engines/(engineId)/visibleSensors

  • Also:
    -deviceId property now becomes serialNumber (for all scan engines).
    - SensorInfo.extra SensorInfo.parameters
/scan/ (engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/outputs/(outputId) shouldRecord


/scan/g2xxx/scanners creationParams.defaultSerialNum int default serial number creation parameter For consistency the creation parameter has been renamed to defaultSerialNumber.
/scan/g2xxx/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors creationParams.serialNum int serial number creation parameter For consistency the creation parameter has been renamed to s erialNumber.


Below is the deprecated content that used to exist on the top-level General page.

This section describes the general protocol definition of the /scan resource, applying to all scan engines. This information can be used toimplement a generic client that works with any scanners, Gocator or 3rd party.

This section does not describe the specifics of the standard scan engines, e.g. g2xxx. This information can be found on Standard Scanner Resources

/scan

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

A collection of scan engines. The list of scan engines is determined by the available scan engines loaded in the system. Engines cannot be created or deleted directly via the protocol.

Properties

None

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns all scan engines as embedded items.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "item": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/g2xxx"
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan"
    }
  }
}

Notifications

None.

/scan/(engineId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

Properties

Item Type Schema Description
id string readonly Unique ID of the scan engine, defined by scan engine plugin. e.g. "g2xxx".
displayName culture-specific string readonly

Display name of the engine, e.g. "G2 Scan Engine".

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the resource.

Returns all scanners as embedded resources.

WIP: "item" will be renamed to "go:scanner", and a new rel type "go:content" will be added, similar to the scanner resource described below.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "item": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/g2xxx"
    }
  },
  "displayName": "G2 Scan Engine",
  "id": "g2xxx"
}

Notifications

Notification
(WIP) created Occurs when a new scan engine has been loaded by the system.
(WIP) deleted Occurs when a scan engine has been unloaded from the system.

/scan/(engineId)/visibleSensors

Resource Type: N/A

Description

Contains information about sensors discovered by the scan engine. All properties on this resource are read-only.

Properties

Item Type Schema Description
sensors SensorInfo[] readonly Array of sensor info objects.

SensorInfo:

Item Type Schema Description
isLocal bool readonly True if the device is local to the application (e.g. running on the sensor). False if the device is remote.
deviceId string readonly

A string that uniquely identifies the device on the network. For a Gocator sensor, it is the serial number (represented as a string).

It should not confused with the "sensor ID" in the scanner/sensors/(sensorId) resource.

model string readonly Model of the sensor. Can be empty if the scan engine does not support discovering the model.
version string readonly Version of the sensor firmware. Can be empty if the scan engine does not support discovering the version.

state

int readonly

Connection state of the sensor.

  • 0 - Connectable.
  • 1 - Connected (by any scanner).
  • -1 - The sensor is currently in a state that does not allow connection.
  • -2 - The sensor is incompatible with the scan engine.
  • -3 - There is a restriction in place that does not allow connection to this sensor.
connectionMsg string readonly

Describes some details about the error if the connection state is error.

Can be empty. The scan engine is not obligated to provide this.

extra ParamSet
Extra information whose format depends on the scan engine. Uses the same parameter format as tools.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the resource.

Example Response: 

{  
  "sensors": [
    {
      "isLocal": true,
      "deviceId": "12345",
      "model": "312340-2M-01",
      "version": "6.1.2.3",
      "connectionState": 1,
      "connectionMsg": "",
      "extra": {
        "optionCode": 1
      }
    },  
    {
      "isLocal": true,
      "deviceId": "29487",
      "model": "312340-2M-01",
      "version": "6.1.2.3",
      "connectionState": 0,
      "connectionMsg": "",
      "extra": {
        "optionCode": 1
      }
    }
  ]
}

Notifications

Notification
updated
Occurs whenever the sensor resource has changed.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

Description

A collection of scanners under the engine.

Properties

Item Type Schema Description
creationParams ParamSet readonly Custom parameters used in the "create" method. These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the resource.

Also returns all scanners as embedded items.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "item": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners"
    }
  },
  "creationParams": {
    "defaultSerialNum": 0
  }
}
create

Creates a scanner resource. A client should read the "scanners" resource to obtain creationParams, update it, and use it in the create method.

Example Request: 

create /scan/g2xxx/scanners {"defaultSerialNum": 12345}

Notifications

Event Type Description
created

Occurs when a new scanner is added. The path indicates the new scanner id.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "created", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0"}
updated

This occurs whenever scanners's properties change. The path indicates the affected scanner id.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "updated", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0" }
deleted

This occurs whenever a scanner is deleted. The path indicates the scanner id that was deleted.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "deleted", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0" }

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID: Auto generated.

Description

A scanner object.

Resource Properties

Item Type Keywords Description
id [readonly] string
The ID within the collection (e.g. "scanner-0").
displayName culture-specific string
User configurable display name of the scanner.
parameters ParamSet
Scanner parameters that varies depending on the scanner type. These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the scanner resource.

Returns child sensor and content resources as embedded items.

Returns actions as links. 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "go:content": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/time"
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/wallboard"
          }
        }
      }
    ],
    "go:sensor": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/default"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "go:action": [
      {
        "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/commands/move"
      },
      {
        "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/commands/ping"
      },
      {
        "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/commands/trigger"
      }
    ],
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0"
    }
  },
  "displayName": "Gocator 0",
  "id": "scanner-0",
  "parameters": {
    // custom parameters
  }
}
update Updates the scanner resource.
delete Deletes the scanner resource.

Notifications

Notification
(WIP) updated
Occurs whenever the scanner resource has changed.
(WIP) created Occurs whenever a scanner resource has been created.
(WIP) deleted Occurs whenever a scanner resource has been deleted.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

A collection of sensors assigned to the scanner.

Item Type Keywords Description
creationParams [readonly] ParamSet
Custom parameters used in the "create" method. These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns all assigned sensors as embedded items as well as creation params. 

{
  "payload": {
    "_embedded": {
      "item": [
        {
          "_links": {
            "self": {
              "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0"
            }
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "_links": {
      "self": {
        "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors"
      }
    },
    "creationParams": {
      "serialNum": 0
    }
  },
  "status": 1,
  "type": "response"
}
create

Method Trait: ( NoRestart ) Only applies for if a scanner has a sensor and is already running (buddying)

Creates a sensor resource. A client should read the "sensors" resource to obtain creationParams, update it, and use it in the create method.

Example Request: 

create /scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors {"serialNum": 12345}

Notifications

None.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors/(sensorId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID: Auto generated.

A sensor object.

Item Type Keywords Description
id [readonly] string
The ID within the collection (e.g. "sensor-0").
displayName culture-specific string
User configurable display name of the sensor.
parameters ParamSet
Sensor parameters that varies depending on the scanner type. These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the sensor resource.

Returns content resources as embedded items.

Returns actions as links.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "go:content": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0/alignment"
          }
        }
      }
    ],
  },
  "_links": {
    "go:actions": [
      {
        "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0/actions/upgrade"
      }
    ],
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0"
    }
  },

  "displayName": "Sensor 0",
  "id": "sensor-0",
  "parameters": {
    // custom parameters
  }
}
update Updates the sensor resource.
delete Deletes the sensor resource.

Notifications

Event Type Description
created

This occurs when a new sensor is added. The path indicates the new sensor id.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "created", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0"}
updated

This occurs whenever the sensor's properties change. The path indicates the affected sensor id.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "updated", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0" }
deleted

This occurs whenever a sensor is deleted. The path indicates the sensor id that was deleted.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "deleted", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0" }

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors/(sensorId)/(contentId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID: Predefined by scan engine.

A custom sensor resource defined by the scan engine. The format is a ParamSet , having the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the custom content.

Example Request: 

read /scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-1/alignment

Example Response: 

{
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-1/alignment"
    }
  },
  "xOffset": 0.0,
  "yOffset": 0.0,
  "zOffset": 0.0,
  "xAngle": 0.0,
  "yAngle": 0.0,
  "zAngle": 0.0
}
(WIP) update

Updates the custom content resource.

Example Request: 

update /scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-1/alignment {...}

Notifications

Notification
(WIP) updated
Occurs whenever the sensor resource has changed.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors/(sensorId)/actions

Resource Type: CollectionItem

A collection of sensor actions defined by the scan engine.

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns a list of actions as embedded resources.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "item": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/1/actions/setAddress"
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/1/actions/upgrade"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/1/actions"
    }
  }
}

Notifications

None.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/sensors/(sensorId)/actions/(actionId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem , Callable

ID: Predefined by scan engine.

A callable action. It can also be read to obtain information about the action.

Item Type Keywords Description
parameters ParamSet

Describes the parameters that the action takes. The client should include the updated parameters when calling the action.

These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the action resource.

Example Response: 

{
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/1/actions/setAddress"
    }
  },
  "id": "move",
  "parameters": {
    "enableDhcp": false,
    "ipAddress": "0.0.0.0",
    "subnetMask": "0.0.0.0",
    "gateway": "0.0.0.0"
  }
}
call

Calls the action resource.

Example Request: 

call /scan/test/scanners/0/sensors/1/actions/setAddress {"enableDhcp": false, ...}

Notifications

None.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/outputs

Resource Type: Collection - Unordered

A collection of outputs of a scanner.

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns all scanner outputs as embedded items.

e.g 

{
  "payload": {
    "_embedded": {
      "item": [
        {
          "_links": {
            "self": {
              "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/outputs/sensor-0:profile"
            }
          }
        },
        {
          "_links": {
            "self": {
              "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/outputs/stamp"
            }
          }
        },
        {
          "_links": {
            "self": {
              "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/outputs/top:profile"
            }
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "_links": {
      "self": {
        "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/outputs"
      }
    }
  },
  "status": 1,
  "type": "response"
}

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/outputs/(outputId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID: Predefined by scan engine.

A specific scanner output that contains information about the output

Item Type Keywords Description
dataFormat [readonly] DataFormat
Data format of the output
dataSourceId [readonly] string
The data source id ( i.e."scan:g2xxx:scanner-0:top:surface")
enabled bool
Whether the output is enabled or not
id [readonly] string
Internal id of the output
displayName culture-specific string
User configurable display name of the scanner output.
recordingEnabled bool
Whether the output should be recorded or not.

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns the output parameters described above. 

{
  "payload": {
    "_links": {
      "self": {
        "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/outputs/stamp"
      }
    },
    "dataFormat": {
      "arrayed": false,
      "type": 8
    },
    "dataSourceId": "scan:g2xxx:scanner-0:stamp",
    "enabled": true,
    "id": "stamp",
    "displayName": "Stamp",
    "recordingEnabled": true
  },
  "status": 1,
  "type": "response"
}
Update updates the output resource.

Notifications

Event Type Description
created

This occurs when a new output is added. The path indicates the new scanner output.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "created", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0/top:surface"}
updated

This occurs whenever the scanner outputs properties change. The path indicates the affected scanner output.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "updated", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0/stamp" }
deleted

This occurs whenever a scanner output is deleted. The path indicates the scanner output that was deleted.

Example: 

{ "eventType" : "deleted", "path" : "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/sensors/sensor-0/top:surface" }

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/(contentId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem

ID: Predefined by scan engine.

A custom scanner resource defined by the scan engine. The format is a ParamSet , having the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the custom content.

Example Request: 

read /scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/layout

Example Response: 

{
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/layout"
    }
  },
  "grid": {
    // ...
  }
}
update

Updates the custom content resource.

Example Request: 

update /scan/g2xxx/scanners/scanner-0/layout {...}

Notifications

Notification
updated
Occurs whenever the scanner resource has changed.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/actions

Resource Type: CollectionItem

A collection of scanner actions defined by the scan engine.

Methods

Method Description
read

Returns a list of commands as embedded resources.

Example Response: 

{
  "_embedded": {
    "item": [
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/actions/move"
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/actions/ping"
          }
        }
      },
      {
        "_links": {
          "self": {
            "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/actions/trigger"
          }
        }
      }
    ]
  },

    ]
  },
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/actions"
    }
  }
}

Notifications

None.

/scan/(engineId)/scanners/(scannerId)/actions/(actionId)

Resource Type: CollectionItem , Callable

ID: Predefined by scan engine.

A scanner action. It can also be read to obtain information about the action.

Item Type Keywords Description
parameters ParamSet

Describes the parameters that the action takes. The client should include the updated parameters when calling the action.

These have the same format as tool parameters.

Methods

Method Description
read

Reads the command resource.

Example Response: 

{
  "_links": {
    "self": {
      "href": "/scan/test/scanners/0/action/move"
    }
  },
  "id": "move",
  "parameters": {
    "objectId": ""
  }
}
call

Calls the action resource.

Example Request: 

call /scan/test/scanners/0/actions/move {"objectId": "a5p1%ak1h9sjh"}

Notifications

None.

/logs

(The following field has been deprecated, and should no longer be referenced beginning with GoPxL BE TBA).

Resource Type: N /A

Item Type Description
args array<string>

Log message arguments.

Note: This is no longer necessary (requirement removed in GOS-3678 - System Logs updates not displayed when using TestValidationTool Complete )
